evals.prompt: ok i just finished planning phase 13 for my saas app. there's a PLAN.md at .planning/phases/13-auth/13-01-PLAN.md with 3 tasks — two backend (JWT auth + refresh tokens) and one frontend (login form). the backend tasks are independent but the frontend depends on both. execute it
evals.expected_output: Should detect GSD mode, find the plan, identify 2 waves (wave 1: 2 parallel backend tasks, wave 2: 1 frontend task), dispatch general-purpose subagents (not gsd-executor), include TDD directives (from internal skills/test-driven-development/) for backend tasks and frontend-design directives (from internal skills/frontend-design/) for the login form, run design gates (/critique then /polish) after frontend task, generate SUMMARY.md, update GSD state, and invoke /review for pre-promotion quality + security + TS strictness checks.

assertions.text: Includes TDD directives in subagent prompts
Runs design gates for frontend task
Invokes /review for pre-promotion checks (not requesting-code-review directly)
Does NOT invoke requesting-code-review or finishing-a-development-branch directly

evals.prompt: build the plan. there's no .planning directory, I just have a plan.md in the root of my project with 2 tasks
evals.expected_output: Should detect that GSD project is missing (.planning/PROJECT.md absent), stop, and tell the user to run /new-project first. Should NOT proceed with execution.

evals.prompt: I need to add stripe subscription billing to my app. users should be able to pick a plan, enter payment, and see their current subscription in settings. I've never used the stripe API before
evals.expected_output: Should detect research-first need (unfamiliar API: Stripe), run research step BEFORE brainstorming (not after), delegate research to subagent with Firecrawl/Context7, then brainstorm with findings, discuss implementation gray areas, derive must_haves with user-facing truths, create PLAN.md with proper frontmatter, and run plan-check verification loop.

assertions.text: Detects research-first need from 'never used Stripe API'

evals.prompt: plan adding a dark mode toggle to the settings page. we already use tailwind's dark: prefix and have a ThemeProvider component
evals.expected_output: Should detect well-known pattern (no research needed), skip research step, proceed directly to brainstorming, note frontend work and read DESIGN.md, produce a small plan (2-3 tasks, 5-8 files), include checkpoint:human-verify for visual verification.

assertions.text: Skips research step for well-known pattern
Notes frontend work and references DESIGN.md

evals.prompt: the login page is showing a blank white screen. no errors in the terminal but the browser console says 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading map)' in AuthProvider.tsx line 42
evals.expected_output: Should triage as SIMPLE (single file, cause visible from error message), skip systematic debugging, go straight to TDD fix (write failing test reproducing the undefined.map error, fix it, refactor), do frontend check since it's a .tsx file, run verification.

evals.prompt: everything is broken after the last deploy. the API returns 500s on /api/users and /api/billing, the dashboard shows stale data, and there's a new TypeScript error in three different service files. I think it might be related to the prisma schema migration I ran yesterday
evals.expected_output: Should triage as PARALLEL (3+ independent failures across subsystems), dispatch parallel agents per subsystem (API users, API billing, dashboard), collect results, TDD fix for each, verify all together.

evals.prompt: hey, picking this back up. where was I?
evals.expected_output: Should read STATE.md, ROADMAP.md, CONCERNS.md, scan for incomplete plans (PLAN without SUMMARY), check git state, present a concise briefing with project name/branch/position/status/last activity/blockers, then route to the right next action.

evals.prompt: just installed fhhs-skills, what do I need to do to get started
evals.expected_output: Should welcome the user with an overview of what fhhs-skills provides (composites, engineering discipline, design quality, code intelligence, project tracking). Should detect platform (macOS/Linux/Windows) and check prerequisites (brew, node, npm, gh, vercel, typescript-language-server, git). Should install missing tools via Homebrew (macOS/Linux) or present winget/installer links (Windows). Should set up TypeScript LSP (npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript + claude plugin install typescript-lsp). Should configure GSD symlink ($HOME/.claude/get-shit-done/bin/). Should present final status report and suggest /new-project, /help, or /resume.

assertions.text: Welcomes user and explains plugin scope

evals.prompt: i want to extract all the inline SQL queries from our route handlers into a separate data access layer. there are about 15 route files that do direct prisma calls
evals.expected_output: Should identify blast radius (15 files, multiple subsystems), suggest writing a PLAN.md via /plan since it's 10+ files, capture baseline tests first, plan atomic steps, and enforce the iron law (tests never go red).

assertions.text: Considers redirecting to /plan for large scope
Enforces iron law (tests never go red)

evals.prompt: verify phase 13
evals.expected_output: Should load all PLAN.md files in .planning/phases/13-*, extract must_haves, run goal-backward verification (truth table + artifacts + key links via gsd-tools), run evidence-based verification (fresh test suite, build check, linter), detect anti-patterns, write VERIFICATION.md, and generate gap-closure plans if anything fails.

assertions.text: Runs goal-backward verification with truth table

evals.prompt: I want to build a recipe sharing app where home cooks can post recipes with photos and others can save favorites. should work on mobile. i'm thinking react
evals.expected_output: Should walk through vision questions one at a time (what/who/why/scope/constraints/success), present default stack (Next.js/TS/Tailwind/Shadcn/Vercel + Supabase since auth/DB needed), invoke /teach-impeccable for design context (output to .planning/DESIGN.md), generate CLAUDE.md under 40 lines, create .planning/ structure with PROJECT.md/REQUIREMENTS.md/ROADMAP.md/STATE.md, and suggest /plan as next step. No external dependency checks needed.

evals.prompt: execute the plan at .planning/phases/05-onboarding/05-02-PLAN.md. the plan has a task with type checkpoint:human-verify for reviewing the welcome screen visually
evals.expected_output: Should find the plan, analyze waves, dispatch subagents for non-checkpoint tasks, and when reaching the checkpoint:human-verify task, the subagent should STOP and return structured state (what was built, verification steps, URLs/commands, expected behavior). Orchestrator should present this to user and WAIT before continuing. Should NOT auto-proceed past the checkpoint. After checkpoint passes, invokes /review for pre-promotion quality and security checks.

assertions.text: Invokes /review after checkpoint passes (not requesting-code-review directly)

evals.prompt: plan adding real-time notifications. we already discussed this in phase 8 and there's a .planning/phases/08-notifications/08-CONTEXT.md with locked decisions about using SSE instead of websockets
evals.expected_output: Should detect existing CONTEXT.md, SKIP Step 3 (Discuss Implementation) since decisions are already locked, read the CONTEXT.md and honor the SSE decision in the plan, and NOT re-open the SSE vs websockets discussion. Plan should reference the locked SSE decision.

evals.prompt: users are reporting intermittent 502 errors on our API but I can't reproduce it locally. it happens maybe once every 50 requests and the logs show different errors each time — sometimes connection reset, sometimes timeout, sometimes a memory warning. no clear pattern
evals.expected_output: Should triage as COMPLEX (unclear cause, needs hypotheses, intermittent, no clear pattern), write triage findings to .planning/debug/YYYY-MM-DD-intermittent-502-errors.md with initial hypotheses, and dispatch a gsd-debugger agent for sustained scientific investigation. Should NOT attempt a quick fix or triage as SIMPLE/MODERATE.

evals.prompt: build the auth phase. there are 3 plans: 05-01 is done (has SUMMARY), 05-02 and 05-03 still need execution
evals.expected_output: Should detect resume scenario (multiple plans, some complete), report 'Found 3 plans, 1 already completed. Continuing from plan 05-02.' Should skip 05-01 entirely. Should check 05-01's SUMMARY for unresolved 'Issues Encountered' and ask user if any found. Should execute 05-02 first, then 05-03 sequentially.

evals.prompt: i want to add a feature for exporting reports as PDF. this doesn't match any phase in the roadmap
evals.expected_output: Should compare against ROADMAP.md phases, detect no match, ask user: 'This doesn't match any existing phase. Add as a new phase to the roadmap, or fold into the current phase?' Should wait for user choice before proceeding. If new phase chosen, should add phase to ROADMAP.md and create phase directory.

assertions.text: Asks user before creating new phase

evals.prompt: run an audit on this project — check accessibility, performance, theming, and responsive design
evals.expected_output: Should detect multiple categories of issues: accessibility (missing ARIA roles on sidebar, toast, comment-card), performance (layout thrashing in chart-widget, client waterfall in page.tsx), design anti-patterns (AI slop in metric-card), responsive (fixed 280px sidebar, 800px min-width on users page), and theming (missing dark mode variable for surface-elevated).

assertions.text: Detects missing ARIA roles (navigation, alert, aria-sort, aria-current)
Detects performance issues (layout thrashing, client waterfall)
Detects AI slop anti-patterns (gradient text, glassmorphism)

evals.prompt: critique the dashboard page. it's at src/app/dashboard/page.tsx
evals.expected_output: Should read skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md for design principles and anti-patterns, then evaluate the dashboard page across all dimensions (AI slop detection, visual hierarchy, information architecture, emotional resonance, etc.). Should produce a structured critique report with Anti-Patterns Verdict, Overall Impression, What's Working, Priority Issues with severity, and specific fix commands (/polish, /simplify, etc.).

evals.prompt: set up the design context for my project
evals.expected_output: Should explore the codebase first (README, package.json, existing components, brand assets), then ask UX-focused questions about what couldn't be inferred. Should write output to .planning/DESIGN.md (NOT CLAUDE.md) with YAML frontmatter (created date, type: design-context) and a Design Context section with Users, Brand Personality, Aesthetic Direction, and Design Principles.

evals.prompt: simplify the changes I just made
evals.expected_output: Should invoke skills/simplify/ which runs 3 parallel review agents on the git diff (code reuse, code quality, efficiency). Should fix issues directly and commit with 'refactor(scope): simplify pass'. Should NOT read full codebase - only operates on changed files from git diff.

assertions.text: Operates on git diff only, not full codebase

evals.prompt: clean up the auth module after refactoring
evals.expected_output: Should invoke skills/simplify/ with the auth module as scope. Should check git diff for recent auth-related changes and run 3 parallel agents. If no git diff exists for auth files, should inform user there are no recent changes to simplify.

assertions.text: Uses git diff to identify changes

evals.prompt: research how to implement real-time collaborative editing like google docs. looking at CRDTs vs OT
evals.expected_output: Should check for .planning/PROJECT.md (GSD dependency). If present, determine scope as Comparison mode (CRDTs vs OT). Should dispatch a Task subagent with Firecrawl/Context7 instructions. Subagent output should go to .planning/phases/XX-name/XX-RESEARCH.md with YAML frontmatter (researched date, domain, confidence). Report should be prescriptive with confidence tags (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW). Should update STATE.md.

assertions.text: Output has YAML frontmatter with confidence

evals.prompt: research best practices for image optimization in Next.js
evals.expected_output: Should check for .planning/PROJECT.md. If missing, should stop and tell user to run /new-project first. Should NOT proceed with research without GSD project.

evals.prompt: check the UI on the settings page at localhost:3000/settings
evals.expected_output: Should check for dev server on common ports. Should check agent-browser availability. If agent-browser not installed, should stop and tell user to install it. If available, should capture screenshots at 3 viewports (desktop 1440x900, tablet 768x1024, mobile 375x812), run console health check, read .planning/DESIGN.md for design context, perform design critique with severity ratings, and produce a Visual Verification Report.

evals.prompt: verify the dashboard looks right. agent-browser is not installed on this machine
evals.expected_output: Should detect that agent-browser is not available (via which check), stop, and tell the user to install it with 'npm install -g agent-browser'. Should NOT attempt to proceed without it or use alternative tools.

evals.prompt: progress
evals.expected_output: Should run gsd-tools.cjs init progress, load roadmap analysis and state snapshot, gather recent SUMMARY.md files, present rich status report with progress bar, recent work, current position, key decisions, blockers, pending todos, and route to next action (Route A if unexecuted plans exist, Route B if phase needs planning, Route C/D/E/F for other states).

assertions.text: Shows recent work from SUMMARY.md files

evals.prompt: progress (no .planning/ directory exists)
evals.expected_output: Should detect project_exists is false from gsd-tools init, output 'No planning structure found.' and suggest running /new-project. Should NOT attempt to read STATE.md or ROADMAP.md.

assertions.text: Does NOT attempt to read non-existent files

evals.prompt: progress (all phases in current milestone are complete)
evals.expected_output: Should detect milestone completion (current phase = highest phase), show Route D with milestone complete message, suggest /verify to verify and complete the milestone. Should display the completion celebration format.

evals.prompt: progress (ROADMAP.md missing but PROJECT.md exists — milestone was archived)
evals.expected_output: Should detect between-milestones state (Route F), read MILESTONES.md for last completed version, suggest /plan for next milestone. Should NOT suggest /new-project since PROJECT.md exists.

evals.prompt: quick add a loading spinner to the submit button in the contact form
evals.expected_output: Should parse arguments (no flags), use description 'add a loading spinner to the submit button in the contact form', run gsd-tools init quick, create .planning/quick/NNN-slug/ directory, spawn gsd-planner in quick mode (not quick-full), spawn gsd-executor, update STATE.md Quick Tasks Completed table, commit artifacts, show completion banner.

evals.prompt: quick --discuss --full implement rate limiting on the /api/upload endpoint
evals.expected_output: Should parse both flags (--discuss and --full). Should show DISCUSS + FULL banner. In discuss phase: identify 2-4 gray areas about rate limiting (e.g., rate limits per user/IP, response when limited, storage for counters). Present gray areas via AskUserQuestion with multiSelect. Capture decisions in CONTEXT.md. Then spawn gsd-planner in quick-full mode with CONTEXT.md reference. Run plan-checker loop (max 2 iterations). Spawn gsd-executor. Run verification via gsd-verifier. Update STATE.md with Status column. Show completion banner with verification status.

assertions.text: Identifies domain-specific gray areas for rate limiting

evals.prompt: quick --full rename the UserProfile component to AccountProfile everywhere
evals.expected_output: Should parse --full flag. Spawn gsd-planner in quick-full mode (must generate must_haves). Run plan-checker. Spawn gsd-executor. Run gsd-verifier. CONTEXT.md should NOT be created (no --discuss). STATE.md should have Status column.

evals.prompt: quick fix the typo in the footer (no .planning/ROADMAP.md exists)
evals.expected_output: Should detect ROADMAP.md is missing from gsd-tools init quick response (roadmap_exists=false). Should error and tell user that quick mode requires an active project with ROADMAP.md. Should suggest /new-project. Should NOT proceed.

evals.prompt: health
evals.expected_output: Should run gsd-tools.cjs validate health, parse JSON output with status/errors/warnings/info, format output with banner (GSD Health Check), show status (HEALTHY/DEGRADED/BROKEN), list any errors with codes (E001-E005) and warnings (W001-W009). If repairable issues exist, show footer with 'Run: health --repair'.

assertions.text: Shows repair suggestion if repairable issues exist

evals.prompt: health --repair
evals.expected_output: Should run gsd-tools.cjs validate health --repair. Should show repairs performed (e.g., createConfig, resetConfig, regenerateState, addNyquistKey). Should re-run health check after repairs to confirm issues resolved. Should show final status.

evals.prompt: revise-claude-md
evals.expected_output: Should detect Session Learnings mode (no argument). Should reflect on session for missing context (bash commands, code style, testing approaches, environment quirks). Should find and read CLAUDE.md files. Should check for GSD project and DESIGN.md. Should read update-guidelines.md for rules. Should draft additions (one line per concept). Should show proposed changes with diff format and 'Why' rationale. Should ask for approval before applying. Should commit with 'docs: update CLAUDE.md with session learnings'.

assertions.text: Shows proposed changes in diff format

evals.prompt: revise-claude-md audit
evals.expected_output: Should detect Full Audit mode. Should invoke skills/claude-md-improver/ completely. The improver skill handles discovery, quality assessment, scoring, and targeted updates. Should NOT duplicate the improver's work inline.

assertions.text: Detects Full Audit mode from 'audit' argument

evals.prompt: revise-claude-md init
evals.expected_output: Should detect Initial Creation mode. Should read project context from /new-project steps (project name, tech stack, design system). Should read templates.md from claude-md-improver references. Should use the fhhs-skills Project template. Should generate CLAUDE.md under 40 lines with project name, tech stack, commands, architecture, code style, testing, planning, and gotchas sections. Should commit with 'docs: initialize CLAUDE.md with project conventions'.

evals.prompt: map-codebase
evals.expected_output: Should run gsd-tools.cjs init map-codebase, create .planning/codebase/ directory, spawn 4 parallel gsd-codebase-mapper agents (tech, arch, quality, concerns) with run_in_background=true. Agents write documents directly (orchestrator does NOT receive document contents). Should verify all 7 documents created (STACK.md, INTEGRATIONS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, STRUCTURE.md, CONVENTIONS.md, TESTING.md, CONCERNS.md). Should scan for secrets before committing. Should commit and show completion summary with line counts.

assertions.text: Shows completion summary with line counts

evals.prompt: map-codebase (codebase already mapped, .planning/codebase/ exists)
evals.expected_output: Should detect existing .planning/codebase/ from init context (has_maps=true). Should list existing documents. Should offer 3 choices: Refresh (delete and remap), Update (selectively update), Skip (use existing). Should wait for user choice before proceeding.

evals.prompt: help
evals.expected_output: Should output ONLY the reference content from the command — the command tables for Build Pipeline, Engineering, Navigation, Design Quality, Task Management, Setup & Maintenance, plus Common Workflows, Files & Structure, Architecture, Non-Negotiable Disciplines, and Code Intelligence sections. Should NOT add project-specific analysis, git status, next-step suggestions, or any commentary beyond the reference.

assertions.text: Includes all 6 command categories (Build Pipeline, Engineering, Navigation, Design Quality, Task Management, Setup & Maintenance)
Includes Architecture and Non-Negotiable Disciplines sections
No git status or suggestions added

evals.prompt: critique this metric card component from a design perspective
evals.expected_output: Should identify AI slop tells: gradient text effect, glassmorphism backdrop-blur, oversized text-5xl hero numbers, decorative SVG blobs. Should recommend simpler, data-focused design that lets the numbers speak.

assertions.text: Identifies gradient text as AI slop anti-pattern
Recommends simpler presentation focused on data clarity

evals.prompt: add-todo The modal z-index is wrong on the billing page, it shows behind the sidebar
evals.expected_output: Should create .planning/todos/pending/ and .planning/todos/done/ if missing. Should extract content from arguments. Should infer area as 'ui' (modal, z-index, sidebar are UI concerns). Should check for duplicate todos. Should create file at .planning/todos/pending/YYYY-MM-DD-modal-z-index-billing.md with frontmatter (created, title, area: ui, files). Should update STATE.md todo count. Should commit.

evals.prompt: add-todo
evals.expected_output: Should detect no arguments provided. Should extract task from current conversation context (what was just discussed). If conversation has no clear task context, should prompt user interactively for the todo description.

assertions.text: Attempts to extract from conversation context
Falls back to interactive prompt if needed

evals.prompt: check-todos
evals.expected_output: Should initialize todo context via gsd-tools. Should list all pending todos from .planning/todos/pending/ with relative timestamps. Should allow user to select a todo. Should load selected todo's full context. Should check ROADMAP.md for phase match. Should offer actions: work on it, add to phase plan, create phase, brainstorm, put back. Should execute chosen action and update STATE.md.

evals.prompt: settings
evals.expected_output: Should present current configuration from .planning/config.json. Should show options for model profile (quality/balanced/budget), workflow toggles (research, plan_check, verifier, auto_advance, nyquist_validation), and git branching (none/per-phase/per-milestone). Should save changes to .planning/config.json. Should offer to save as global defaults to ~/.gsd/defaults.json.

evals.prompt: build the plan (plan has only frontend tasks - React component refactor, new page layout, CSS updates)
evals.expected_output: Should detect all tasks are frontend. Should dispatch subagents with frontend-design directives from skills/frontend-design/. Should run design gates after execution (/critique -> /polish -> /normalize). Should read .planning/DESIGN.md for design context during critique. Should check for frontend anti-patterns (AI slop, emoji in UI, generic gradients).

assertions.text: Includes frontend-design directives in subagent prompts
Runs design gates: critique -> polish -> normalize

evals.prompt: the CSS grid layout breaks on tablet. elements overlap at 768px width
evals.expected_output: Should triage as MODERATE (2-4 files, single subsystem - CSS/layout). Should use systematic debugging to identify the breakpoint issue. Should apply TDD fix. Should detect frontend work and check for design system consistency. Should run /simplify pass (MODERATE triggers simplify). Should verify fix at the specific breakpoint.

evals.prompt: verify (no phase specified, working on current phase from STATE.md)
evals.expected_output: Should detect scope from STATE.md current position (no explicit phase argument). Should load PLAN.md files from the current phase directory. Should run full dual verification. Should write VERIFICATION.md in the phase directory. If frontend work detected, should suggest /verify-ui.

evals.prompt: progress (phase has UAT.md with status: diagnosed and gaps needing fixes)
evals.expected_output: Should detect UAT gaps (uat_with_gaps > 0) and show Route E. Should display UAT Gaps Found message with gap count. Should suggest /plan to plan fixes for the gaps. Should suggest /clear first for fresh context.

assertions.text: Detects UAT gaps from diagnosed UAT.md

evals.prompt: quick --discuss add webhook support for new user signups
evals.expected_output: Should parse --discuss flag (no --full). Should show DISCUSS banner. Should identify gray areas specific to webhooks (e.g., payload format, retry policy, authentication/signing, which events to support). Should present gray areas via AskUserQuestion with multiSelect. Should capture decisions in CONTEXT.md. Should spawn gsd-planner in standard quick mode (NOT quick-full). Should NOT run plan-checker or verifier (no --full). STATE.md table should NOT have Status column.

evals.prompt: research what auth providers are available for Next.js. we need social login and magic links
evals.expected_output: Should check GSD project exists. Should identify Ecosystem research mode ('what exists?'). Should dispatch Task subagent with instructions for Firecrawl/Context7. Should produce prescriptive output ('Use X because Y', not 'Consider X or Y'). Should tag findings with confidence levels. Should save to .planning/phases/XX-name/XX-RESEARCH.md.

evals.prompt: I want to create a CLI tool for managing dotfiles. pure node.js, no framework needed, just a simple npm package
evals.expected_output: Should walk through vision questions. Should NOT suggest Next.js/React/Tailwind defaults (this is a CLI tool, not a web app). Should adapt stack to CLI context (Node.js, Commander/yargs, minimal deps). Should still invoke /teach-impeccable but adapted for CLI (not visual design). Should create .planning/ structure. CLAUDE.md should reflect CLI-specific conventions (not web conventions).

assertions.text: Adapts stack for CLI (no web framework defaults)
Does not suggest React/Tailwind for CLI tool

evals.prompt: polish the settings page at src/app/settings/page.tsx
evals.expected_output: Should read the file, check .planning/DESIGN.md for design context, then fix alignment, spacing, consistency, and detail issues. Should commit changes.

evals.prompt: harden the checkout form at src/components/CheckoutForm.tsx — make it handle edge cases
evals.expected_output: Should read the component, add error handling for failed payments, handle empty/invalid inputs, add loading states, handle network errors gracefully. Should check i18n readiness.

assertions.text: Adds error handling for edge cases

evals.prompt: adapt the dashboard layout to work on mobile. currently it's desktop-only with a sidebar
evals.expected_output: Should analyze current layout, add responsive breakpoints, convert sidebar to mobile-friendly navigation (bottom nav or hamburger), ensure touch targets are 44px+, test at 375px width.

evals.prompt: add animations to the modal component at src/components/Modal.tsx — it just snaps open/closed
evals.expected_output: Should add entrance/exit animations (fade + scale or slide), use CSS transitions or framer-motion, respect prefers-reduced-motion, keep animations under 300ms for responsiveness.

evals.prompt: distill the user profile page — it has too many sections and options, feels overwhelming
evals.expected_output: Should analyze the page, identify low-value sections, consolidate related settings, apply progressive disclosure (hide advanced options), reduce visual noise.

evals.prompt: normalize the buttons across the app — some are rounded, some square, inconsistent padding
evals.expected_output: Should scan for button variants, identify inconsistencies against the design system or established patterns, standardize border-radius, padding, font-size, and color usage.

evals.prompt: audit the landing page at src/app/page.tsx
evals.expected_output: Should evaluate across accessibility (WCAG compliance, alt texts, keyboard nav), performance (image optimization, lazy loading), theming (dark mode support), and responsive design. Should produce a severity-rated report.

evals.prompt: design an onboarding flow for new users — right now they just land on an empty dashboard after signup
evals.expected_output: Should design a multi-step onboarding: welcome screen, key feature highlights, first action prompt (e.g., create first project), progress indicator. Should handle empty states with helpful CTAs.

evals.prompt: update
evals.expected_output: Should check current plugin version against latest available version. If update available, should download and install. Should clear statusline cache after updating. Should report what changed (version diff).

evals.prompt: Build the auth plan at .planning/phases/01-auth/01-01-PLAN.md. It has 3 tasks — JWT auth middleware, refresh token rotation (both backend, independent), and a login form that depends on both.
evals.expected_output: Should detect GSD mode, find the plan, identify 2 waves (wave 1: 2 parallel backend tasks, wave 2: 1 frontend task). Dispatch general-purpose subagents with Next.js performance rules in prompts (waterfall avoidance, bundle awareness). Spec gates run per wave. Design gates (critique → polish → normalize) run for the frontend login form task. Simplify pass runs after all waves. Then invoke /review which runs quality review (code-reviewer agent), security scan (4 parallel agents), TS strictness check (any usage in diff), gate decision, and promote. SUMMARY.md generated with correct frontmatter. PR title follows conventional commit format.

assertions.text: Loads Next.js performance rules into subagent prompts (references waterfall or bundle patterns)
Spec gates run BEFORE design gates
/review runs quality → security → gate → promote in sequence
Dispatches code-reviewer agent for quality review
Dispatches 4 parallel agents for security scan
/review runs evidence verification (tests, build, lint with fresh output and exit codes) before gate decision
Checks for any usage in diff (TS strictness)
PR title follows type(scope): summary format
Does NOT invoke requesting-code-review directly (uses /review instead)
Does NOT invoke finishing-a-development-branch directly (uses /review instead)
Frontend login form task gets DESIGN.md context in subagent prompt
Design gates run for frontend task (critique → polish → normalize)
GSD state updated in /build Step 6 BEFORE /review invocation

evals.prompt: Execute the API refactor plan at .planning/phases/02-api/02-01-PLAN.md
evals.expected_output: Should build the API refactor plan. During /review, security scan finds SQL injection via string concatenation (CRITICAL) and hardcoded JWT secret (CRITICAL) in existing code. CRITICAL findings BLOCK promotion. Fix agent is dispatched to remediate CRITICAL findings (parameterized queries, env-based secrets). After fix, re-scan verifies remediation. MEDIUM findings (missing CORS) are logged but do not block. Promotion proceeds after re-scan passes.

assertions.text: Identifies SQL string concatenation as CRITICAL
Identifies hardcoded JWT secret as CRITICAL
Dispatches fix agent to remediate CRITICAL findings
Review report includes security findings with file:line references
MEDIUM findings (missing CORS) logged but do not block promotion
Fix agent replaces SQL concatenation with parameterized queries

evals.prompt: Build the dashboard plan at .planning/phases/02-dashboard/02-01-PLAN.md
evals.expected_output: Should identify 2 waves. Wave 1 is the component task (no Playwright involvement) — subagent prompt should NOT include Playwright guidance. Wave 2 is the Playwright test task — subagent detects *.spec.* files in task and loads Playwright guidance (Page Object Model, locator strategies, auto-waiting). Spec gates run per wave. /review runs after both waves. SUMMARY.md notes Playwright tests written.

assertions.text: Wave 1 subagent prompt does NOT include Playwright guidance
Wave 2 subagent prompt includes Playwright guidance (detects *.spec.* in task files)
Playwright prompt references Page Object Model and locator strategies
Playwright detection happens per-task, not globally at plan level
TDD directive references Playwright patterns for test task
Design gates run for component task in wave 1 (frontend)

evals.prompt: Build the auth plan at .planning/phases/01-auth/01-01-PLAN.md
evals.expected_output: Should build the auth plan. During spec gate, the gate catches any usage in the subagent output (e.g., function params typed as any). This is flagged as BLOCKING — not advisory. Fix agent replaces any with specific types (unknown + type guards, or concrete interface types). Fix happens before the next wave starts. as any assertions are also rejected. Suggests unknown or proper type guard as alternatives.

assertions.text: Spec gate checks for any type usage in subagent output
Flags any as BLOCKING (not just advisory)
Fix agent replaces any with specific type (unknown + type guard or concrete type)
Fix happens before next wave starts
Does not accept as any assertions
Suggests type guard or unknown as alternatives to any
Non-exhaustive switch violations in existing code flagged

evals.prompt: The user profile page crashes when the user has no avatar — TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'url'). It's in src/components/profile.tsx
evals.expected_output: Should triage as MODERATE (single component, one subsystem). Invokes systematic debugging to locate null reference in profile.tsx (accessing avatar.url when avatar is null). TDD approach: writes failing test first reproducing the null avatar case, then fixes with optional chaining or null check, confirms test passes. Frontend check reads DESIGN.md for fallback avatar styling. Simplify runs before /review. /review runs quality review on the fix diff, security scan on changed files, TS strictness check. Fix commit uses fix(profile): prefix. Does NOT fix unrelated pre-existing issues.

assertions.text: Triages as MODERATE (2-4 files, one subsystem)
Writes failing test BEFORE fix (TDD red-green-refactor)
Frontend check reads DESIGN.md for fallback styling
/review runs quality review on fix diff
/review runs security scan on changed files
/review checks for any in diff (TS strictness)
Fix commit message uses fix(scope): prefix
Does NOT fix unrelated pre-existing issues
Null reference fix uses optional chaining or type guard (not as any)

evals.prompt: Refactor the auth module to extract token management into a separate service. src/lib/auth.ts currently handles JWT signing, verification, session management, and refresh token rotation — split token concerns out into src/lib/token-service.ts
evals.expected_output: Should scope with LSP findReferences to map blast radius (all files importing from auth.ts). Captures baseline test suite (must be GREEN). Plans atomic steps ordered safest-first (create new file, move functions, update imports, remove old code). Executes with tests green at every step — reverts immediately if tests go red. Simplify pass after completion. /review runs quality review (behavior preservation focus), security scan (extracted auth service), TS strictness check, and if Next.js files touched, perf patterns. Atomic commits with refactor(auth): prefix.

assertions.text: Uses LSP findReferences to scope blast radius of auth.ts
Captures baseline test suite and verifies GREEN
Reverts immediately if tests go red (iron law)
/review security scan checks extracted auth service
/review checks for any introduced during refactoring
If Next.js files touched, review includes perf patterns
Tests remain green at every atomic step

evals.prompt: Scan the whole codebase for security issues
evals.expected_output: Should scope the full codebase and dispatch exactly 4 parallel scanning agents. Agent findings: SQL injection via string concatenation in api/users/route.ts (CRITICAL), XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML in comment.tsx (CRITICAL), hardcoded JWT secret in auth.ts (CRITICAL), session stored in localStorage (HIGH), .env.example with real-looking API keys (CRITICAL), missing CORS on data route (MEDIUM), no auth check on data route (HIGH). Report has file:line references, findings grouped by severity then category. Overlapping findings from different agents are deduplicated.

assertions.text: Finds SQL injection via string concatenation (CRITICAL) in api/users/route.ts
Finds XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML with user input (CRITICAL) in comment.tsx
Finds hardcoded JWT secret (CRITICAL) in auth.ts
Finds missing CORS (MEDIUM) and no auth (HIGH) on data route
Finds localStorage session storage (HIGH) in auth.ts
Report has file:line references for each finding
Findings grouped by severity then category
Deduplicates overlapping findings from different agents

evals.prompt: Review my changes before creating a PR
evals.expected_output: Should run the full /review pipeline: code quality review first (code-reviewer agent), then security scan (4 parallel agents), then TS strictness check (any usage in diff). Gate decision aggregates all findings. If Next.js project detected, includes perf patterns in review. Review report includes quality score, security findings, and TS strictness results. Presents promotion options (PR/merge/keep/discard from finishing-a-development-branch). PR title enforces conventional commit format.

assertions.text: Security scan runs second (4 parallel agents)
Runs evidence verification (tests, build, lint with fresh output and exit codes) before gate decision
Gate decision aggregates quality + security + TS strictness results
Checks diff for any usage (TS strictness)
Detects Next.js project and includes perf patterns in review
Review report includes quality score, security findings, TS strictness
PR title enforces conventional commit format type(scope): summary
/review does NOT touch STATE.md (GSD state updates happen in /build)

evals.prompt: Release a new version
evals.expected_output: Should gather changes from git log since last tag, classify conventional commit types (feat, fix, refactor, chore, docs). Suggest version bump based on changes (feat → minor, fix → patch, breaking → major). Generate conventional changelog with groups: feat → Added, fix → Fixed, refactor → Changed. Bump both plugin.json and marketplace.json to the same version. Create commit + tag + push + GitHub release. Internal chore/docs commits excluded from user-facing changelog. Asks user for version bump approval before proceeding.

assertions.text: Parses conventional commit types from git log
Both plugin.json and marketplace.json get same version
GitHub release notes include changelog content
Does NOT include internal chore/docs commits in user-facing changelog
Asks user for version bump approval before proceeding

evals.prompt: Build the plan at plan.md in the project root
evals.expected_output: Should detect that .planning/PROJECT.md is missing (no GSD project initialized). Stops immediately and tells the user to run /fh:new-project first. Does NOT proceed with any execution — no subagent dispatch, no plan parsing, no wave analysis.

assertions.text: Does NOT proceed with any execution

evals.prompt: Plan and build adding user settings page with a dark mode toggle. The project already has a ThemeProvider and uses Tailwind dark: prefix.
evals.expected_output: Should run /plan-work first: skips research (dark mode toggle is well-known pattern), brainstorms design options (toggle placement, persistence strategy, system preference detection), discusses with user, derives must_haves with user-facing truths, creates PLAN.md with proper frontmatter. Then /build: loads Next.js perf rules, dispatches subagents, spec gate per wave, design gates (critique → polish → normalize) for frontend components. Design gates run after spec gates. Simplify before /review. /review runs full pipeline (quality + security + TS strictness). SUMMARY.md generated with must_haves verification. PR with conventional commit title.

assertions.text: Skips research for well-known pattern (dark mode toggle)
Brainstorms design options for toggle placement and persistence
Creates PLAN.md with proper YAML frontmatter and XML task structure
Build loads Next.js perf rules into subagent prompts
Design gates run (critique → polish → normalize) for frontend components
Design gates run after spec gates
/review runs quality review on full diff
PR with conventional commit title type(scope): summary
ThemeProvider and Tailwind dark: prefix acknowledged in plan context

evals.prompt: API returns 500 on /api/users and /api/billing simultaneously, and the dashboard shows stale data. Three independent failures.
evals.expected_output: Should triage as PARALLEL (3+ independent failures across different subsystems). Dispatches parallel gsd-debugger agents — one per failing subsystem (users API, billing API, dashboard staleness). Each debugger uses scientific method (hypothesis → test → conclude). TDD fix per root cause. Simplify after all fixes. /review security scan catches SQL injection in /api/users route (CRITICAL). BLOCKS on CRITICAL. Fix agent addresses SQL injection. Re-scan after security fix. SUMMARY.md notes all three subsystem fixes plus security remediation.

assertions.text: Dispatches parallel gsd-debugger agents per subsystem
Each debugger uses scientific method (hypothesis → test → conclude)
TDD fix for each root cause
/review security scan catches SQL injection in /api/users
Fix agent addresses CRITICAL finding (SQL injection)
Re-scan after security fix before promoting
SUMMARY.md notes all three subsystem fixes plus security remediation

evals.prompt: normalize this project's components to use the design system consistently
evals.expected_output: Should detect that user-form.tsx uses raw HTML inputs/buttons instead of shadcn Input/Button components, sidebar uses hard-coded hex colors instead of CSS variables, and login page duplicates validation instead of using shared validators.

assertions.text: Detects raw HTML inputs in user-form.tsx that should use shadcn Input
Detects hard-coded color values in sidebar that should use CSS variables
Detects raw <a> tags in sidebar that should use Next.js Link

evals.prompt: optimize performance in this dashboard app
evals.expected_output: Should detect client-side waterfall in page.tsx (sequential useEffect fetches should be parallel server-side), layout thrashing in chart-widget (reading offsetWidth in loop), missing Suspense boundaries, unoptimized images in user-avatar (raw img instead of next/image), and search input in data-table triggering full re-renders without debounce.

assertions.text: Detects client-side waterfall (sequential fetches in useEffect)
Detects layout thrashing (offsetWidth reads in loop)
Detects missing debounce on data-table search despite hook existing

evals.prompt: harden the user-facing components for edge cases and error handling
evals.expected_output: Should detect missing error state rendering in user-form, missing keyboard dismiss on notification-toast, toast stacking without limit, generic error messages in login page, hard-coded English strings (i18n concern), no empty state for users page, and missing loading states.

assertions.text: Detects missing error states in user-form (validates but doesn't show errors)
Detects missing keyboard dismiss and auto-dismiss on toasts
Detects hard-coded strings as i18n concern

evals.prompt: refactor this codebase — there's a 180-line component and a kitchen sink utility file that need breaking up
evals.expected_output: Should identify data-table.tsx as a god component (180+ lines mixing search, sort, pagination, rendering) and recommend splitting. Should identify utils.ts as kitchen-sink (cn() plus 5 unrelated functions) and recommend extracting. Should identify duplicated fetch pattern in api.ts and recommend shared wrapper.

assertions.text: Identifies data-table as god component needing decomposition
Identifies utils.ts as kitchen-sink needing extraction
Identifies duplicated validation in user-form and login page

evals.prompt: the data-table test is failing, can you fix it?
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose the prop interface mismatch: data-table.test.tsx passes `columns` prop but the DataTable component expects `fields`. Should fix the test to use the correct prop name, not change the component interface. Should follow TDD discipline.

assertions.text: Diagnoses columns→fields prop rename as root cause
Fixes the test, not the component interface
References or resolves todo-003 about this regression

evals.prompt: polish the components in this dashboard for shipping quality
evals.expected_output: Should detect spacing/alignment inconsistencies, missing focus states, inconsistent border-radius usage, hard-coded z-index values (z-[9999] on toast), and color inconsistencies between components using design tokens vs hard-coded values.

assertions.text: Detects inconsistent use of design tokens vs hard-coded colors

evals.prompt: find duplicated patterns and extract them into shared components or utilities
evals.expected_output: Should detect duplicated email validation in validators.ts, user-form.tsx, and login/page.tsx. Should detect that user-form bypasses existing shadcn components. Should detect duplicated fetch+error pattern in api.ts.

assertions.text: Detects duplicated email validation across 3 files
Detects duplicated fetch pattern in api.ts

evals.prompt: make this dashboard work on mobile and tablet devices
evals.expected_output: Should detect fixed 280px sidebar width, min-w-[800px] on users page, and lack of responsive breakpoints. Should recommend hamburger menu for mobile sidebar, responsive table layout, and fluid widths.

assertions.text: Detects fixed 280px sidebar width as non-responsive
Detects min-w-[800px] on users page as mobile-hostile
Recommends hamburger/overlay pattern for mobile navigation

evals.prompt: review the e2e tests and suggest improvements following Playwright best practices
evals.expected_output: Should detect CSS selector anti-patterns (.email-input, .btn-primary, .text-red-600), recommend role-based or test-id locators, suggest Page Object Model pattern, and identify missing error state test coverage.

assertions.text: Detects CSS selector usage (.email-input, .btn-primary) as anti-pattern

evals.prompt: where are we on this project? what's done and what's remaining?
evals.expected_output: Should read STATE.md and ROADMAP.md, report Phase 01 (Auth) is COMPLETE with all requirements satisfied, Phase 02 (Dashboard) is IN_PROGRESS with task 1 done and task 2 in-progress, Phases 03-04 are PLANNED. Should summarize overall progress as ~25-30% complete.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies Phase 02 as IN_PROGRESS with task detail

evals.prompt: I'm coming back to this project after a break, where was I?
evals.expected_output: Should restore context from STATE.md showing Phase 02 in-progress, identify 02-01 plan with task 2 in-progress (widget system), mention pending todos (rate limiting, fetch wrapper, data-table regression), and suggest next action.

assertions.text: Identifies Phase 02 task 2 as the current work in progress
Suggests concrete next action (continue task 2 or fix regression)

evals.prompt: verify that Phase 01 (auth) was completed correctly
evals.expected_output: Should compare 01-01-PLAN.md requirements against 01-01-SUMMARY.md evidence, check that claimed files exist, verify R-001/R-002/R-003 are covered, and note any gaps (e.g., auth.test.ts only tests happy path, hardcoded JWT secret is a security issue in the 'completed' phase).

assertions.text: Compares PLAN requirements against SUMMARY evidence
Verifies claimed artifacts exist on disk
Notes security concern in completed auth implementation

evals.prompt: check the health of this project's planning directory
evals.expected_output: Should validate .planning/ structure, confirm PROJECT.md/STATE.md/ROADMAP.md exist and are consistent, check that phase directories have proper PLAN/SUMMARY pairing (01-auth has both, 02-dashboard has PLAN only), verify todos are valid, and report overall health status.

evals.prompt: what todos do I have pending?
evals.expected_output: Should list 3 todos: todo-001 (rate limiting, pending, high priority, security), todo-002 (fetch wrapper, pending, medium, refactor), todo-003 (data-table regression, in-progress, high, bug). Should categorize by priority and suggest which to tackle first.

assertions.text: Lists all 3 todos with correct status and priority
Identifies todo-001 and todo-003 as high priority
Suggests action order based on priority

evals.prompt: the dashboard page has no empty state or loading experience, can you improve the onboarding?
evals.expected_output: Should detect that page.tsx shows generic 'Loading...' text with no skeleton, has no empty state when data is null/empty, and provides no first-time user guidance. Should recommend skeleton loaders, empty state illustrations with CTAs, and progressive disclosure.

assertions.text: Detects missing empty states for zero-data scenarios

evals.prompt: improve the error messages and microcopy on the login page
evals.expected_output: Should detect generic 'Something went wrong' error that doesn't help users, identify that validation messages could be more helpful (e.g., 'Please fill in all fields' is vague), and suggest more actionable, specific error copy.

assertions.text: Identifies 'Something went wrong' as unhelpful generic error
Suggests more specific, actionable error messages
Improves form field labels or placeholder text
